title: Verify VoiceOver accessibility under non-English locales
summary: Extended LocalizationAccessibilityRegression with 4 new test methods (de/ar/ja/ru) plus refactored the English-only method to share a private helper. Each test asserts the root tab bar exposes exactly 5 buttons and every button reports a non-empty XCUIElement.label — the value VoiceOver announces. Combined with the existing English test, 5 representative locales now have maintained non-empty AX-label coverage. Preserved a manifest under automation/proofs/app-localization-voiceover-verification/ explaining the scope, what was claimed, and what was NOT claimed (no real VoiceOver-listening human pass; non-tab-bar surfaces uncovered).
files_changed: docs/workflows/localization-hig-ui-completion.md (LocalizationAccessibilityRegression bullet now lists VoiceOver-coverage locales and points at the new proof)
refactoring: Existing testRootTabsExposeNonEmptyAccessibilityLabelsUnderEnglish now delegates to the new private assertRootTabsExposeNonEmptyAccessibilityLabels(language:, locale:) helper so the new locale tests share the assertion logic. No semantic change for the English test.
rationale_for_chosen_locales: en = source baseline; de = native-reviewed cross-cut; ar = RTL with translated AX labels; ja = CJK katakana; ru = long Cyrillic. Together they cover the four bucket-gate flavours plus the source. Adding more locales multiplies runtime; assertion shape is locale-agnostic so additional value tapers off after one representative per bucket.
not_claimed: a real VoiceOver-listening human pass occurred (XCUITest reads .label, which is what VoiceOver would announce; the proof is build-level, not listening-pass)
non-tab-bar accessibility labels are covered (Today rows, Continue items, train sessions, write notes, etc.)
the labels are idiomatic VoiceOver phrasing (only that they are non-empty)
AccessibilityXL Dynamic Type + VoiceOver interaction is verified (the AccessibilityXL shell-settle tests are separate)

the 13 uncovered non-English locales (nl, fr, it, ko, nb, pt, pt-BR, es, sv, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, tr, uk, vi) have non-empty-AX-label regression coverage (they don't here; the assertion shape would generalize trivially if a future slice adds them)
residual_risk: iPad and macOS VoiceOver behavior is not covered (iPhone 17 simulator only).

If a future regression adds an accessibilityLabel override that translates to an empty string in a specific locale, the test would fail only if that locale is one of the 5 covered. Adding more locales to the maintained set is cheap (one test method each) when the need surfaces.

XCUIElement.label sometimes falls back to the visible-text label rather than an explicit accessibilityLabel(...). The non-empty assertion catches the empty case but does not distinguish 'natural label fallback' from 'explicit override that says the same thing'.
